evals.prompt: hey can you check PR #243 in this repo? i need to know if jacek left any inline review comments and what they say. also show me which CI checks are still running or failed
evals.expected_output: Should retrieve inline review comments using gh api (not gh pr view --json reviews), extract the hostname and repo path correctly, and check CI status using statusCheckRollup

assertions.name: Uses gh api for inline comments
assertions.description: Should use 'gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/243/comments' to get inline review comments, not 'gh pr view --json reviews' (which returns empty bodies for inline comments)

assertions.name: Correctly identifies repository path
assertions.description: Should use 'gh repo view --json nameWithOwner' or similar to get the correct owner/repo path (vxflexos/agentic_root_cause_analyzer), not guess or use wrong paths like 'eos2git/jarvis'

assertions.name: Handles enterprise GitHub hostname
assertions.description: Should include --hostname flag for the gh api call to eos2git.cec.lab.emc.com, or rely on auto-detection from git remote

assertions.name: Retrieves CI status correctly
assertions.description: Should use 'gh pr view 243 --json statusCheckRollup' or 'gh pr checks 243' to get CI check status

assertions.name: Finds Jacek's comments
assertions.description: Output should contain the actual inline review comments from Jacek-Kreft (there are 2 comments about moving code to top of file)

assertions.name: Reports CI check status
assertions.description: Output should show which checks are passing/failing/in progress

evals.prompt: i just pushed some changes to fix the issues from the code review. can you create a PR for me? title should be 'fix(api): address review feedback' and in the description mention that i fixed the validation logic and added tests. make it a draft PR and request reviews from tal-afek and Jacek-Kreft
evals.expected_output: Should create a draft PR with proper title, multi-line body, and request reviews from specified users

assertions.name: Uses gh pr create command
assertions.description: Should use 'gh pr create' (not gh api) for creating a PR

assertions.name: Includes --draft flag
assertions.description: Command should include --draft flag to create a draft PR

assertions.name: Sets correct title
assertions.description: Command should include --title 'fix(api): address review feedback'

assertions.name: Includes multi-line body
assertions.description: Command should include --body with a description mentioning validation logic and tests, likely using heredoc syntax for multi-line text

assertions.name: Requests reviews from both users
assertions.description: Command should include --reviewer tal-afek,Jacek-Kreft or separate --reviewer flags for each

assertions.name: Does not actually execute the command
assertions.description: Should show the command but not execute it (as instructed)

evals.prompt: there's this PR https://eos2git.cec.lab.emc.com/vxflexos/agentic_root_cause_analyzer/pull/243 and i want to see what files changed and if there are any test failures in the CI. can you show me the diff for any files in src/agentic_root_cause_analyzer/api/ and tell me which checks failed
evals.expected_output: Should handle enterprise GitHub URL, view changed files, filter for specific directory, and identify failed CI checks

assertions.name: Handles enterprise GitHub URL
assertions.description: Should correctly parse the enterprise GitHub URL and work with the eos2git.cec.lab.emc.com instance

assertions.name: Lists changed files
assertions.description: Should use 'gh pr view 243 --json files' or similar to get the list of changed files

assertions.name: Filters for api directory
assertions.description: Output should show only files in src/agentic_root_cause_analyzer/api/ directory

assertions.name: Shows diff for filtered files
assertions.description: Should use 'gh pr diff 243' with file path filtering to show diffs for api directory files

assertions.name: Checks CI status
assertions.description: Should use 'gh pr view 243 --json statusCheckRollup' or 'gh pr checks 243' to get CI check status

assertions.name: Identifies failed checks
assertions.description: Output should clearly indicate which CI checks failed (if any) or that all passed
